title: Coelopleurum brevicaule
intro: Coelopleurum brevicaule, also known as Japanese angelica, is a perennial plant species in the carrot family (Apiaceae), native to China, Japan, and the Russian Far East. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities, which may help alleviate symptoms of colds, fever, and digestive issues. Bioactive constituents of Coelopleurum brevicaule include volatile oils, flavonoids, and lignans, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. The plant is commonly used in traditional herbal medicine and is often prepared as a decoction, infusion, or dried root extract, which is used to treat a range of health conditions.
taxonomy: Coelopleurum brevicaule belongs to the family Apiaceae. It is a genus of flowering plants native to East Asia. The classification of Coelopleurum brevicaule also includes its order, which is Apiales, and its class, which is Magnoliopsida. Within the plant kingdom, Coelopleurum brevicaule is classified under the kingdom Plantae. The species is further classified as a perennial herb.
